description: Create a content slide with the headline 'Is this picture real?' in a two-column text layout, summarizing consumer trust concerns around AI-generated imagery and online content authenticity.

left.body: A Getty Images report revealed that people feel less favorably about brands that use AI-generated visuals. The survey of over 7,500 people from 25 countries found that 90% of consumers want to know if an image is AI-generated, and 87% value image authenticity. Crucially, 76% say they find it increasingly difficult to tell the difference between real and AI-generated images, fueling skepticism.

right.body: Language is evolving to lend expression to the uncomfortable feeling of spotting generative AI content that feels inhuman. “Slop” is the new “spam”—a broad term that has gained traction to describe shoddy or unwanted AI-generated content in art, books, social media and search results. The issue of trust throughout the online experience is multi-faceted and stems from a spectrum of motivations from well-intended and genuine to malicious and harmful.
